name: Suite 5 — Multi-turn refinement
description: Canonical shape 11 (multi-turn refinement). Uses CopilotTestCase.SeedHistory to pre-populate prior chat turns under the same SessionId, then asks a follow-up. The orchestrator's RefinementDetector + SpecMemory should detect the refinement and modify the prior spec rather than emit a fresh one. This is the fine-tuning impact measurement baseline — DO NOT change these cases between pre-train and post-train runs. Pre-train pass rate sits at ~1/8 (model ceiling); LoRA on local 7B should lift to ~5/8.

Scenarios.Question: only the critical ones
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND p.Name = 'Critical' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
Scenarios._note: Narrow refinement — add predicate without losing prior filter.

Scenarios.Question: only from this month

SeedHistory.Content: list tickets created by user admin
Returned the tickets created by admin.

Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId WHERE u.UserName = 'admin' AND t.CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) AND t.IsDeleted = 0
Scenarios._note: Narrow refinement — temporal predicate added to existing user filter.

Scenarios.Question: also include closed ones
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
Scenarios._note: Widen refinement — drop the filter that previously narrowed the result.

Scenarios.Question: what about closed ones
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Closed' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
Scenarios._note: Switch-filter refinement — replace 'Open' filter with 'Closed', keep everything else.

Scenarios.Question: now break that down by priority
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
Scenarios._note: Group-by refinement — add GROUP BY + COUNT to a list query.

Scenarios.Question: now by entity
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT e.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
Scenarios._note: Group-by refinement — convert a scalar count into a grouped count.

Scenarios.Question: sort them by oldest first
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt ASC
Scenarios._note: Order-by refinement — add ORDER BY to prior query.

Scenarios.Question: actually show me the users instead
Scenarios._note: Entity-switch refinement — abandon prior spec, start a fresh one on a different table. Easiest case in the suite (typically the only one a stock 7B gets right).
